title: Taiga
description: Open-source agile project management platform with Scrum and Kanban boards, backlogs, sprints, and issue tracking.
installation: This template deploys taiga-back (the Django API server) ONLY. Taiga is a multi-container stack and is NOT functional without its companion services, which must be deployed separately as their own apps/sidecars: (1) taiga-async = same image taigaio/taiga-back:latest with command celery worker (the background task/Celery worker, shares POSTGRES_* + RABBITMQ + TAIGA_SECRET_KEY); (2) taiga-front = taigaio/taiga-front:latest (the web UI, served on port 80); (3) taiga-events = taigaio/taiga-events:latest (websocket push server, needs its own RabbitMQ broker + the same TAIGA_SECRET_KEY); (4) taiga-protected = taigaio/taiga-protected:latest (signed-URL attachment proxy, needs TAIGA_SECRET_KEY); (5) taiga-gateway = nginx:1.19-alpine routing front/back/events/protected on port 9000 (the real public entrypoint). This template provisions the shared postgres and rabbitmq add-ons that taiga-back, taiga-async, and taiga-events all connect to. ROTATE: TAIGA_SECRET_KEY (set to replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-32 here; generate with `openssl rand -hex 32` and use the SAME value across back/async/events/protected), the postgres password, and the RABBITMQ_PASS. FIRST RUN: exec into taiga-back and run `python manage.py migrate` then `python manage.py loaddata initial_project_templates` and create a superuser with `python manage.py createsuperuser`. Note EVENTS_PUSH_BACKEND_URL and CELERY_BROKER_URL point at the rabbitmq add-on (amqp://taiga:taiga@taiga-rabbitmq:5672).
